content:
Character: Claire Donovan
Perception: Claire arrives at 6:45 nearly every morning — cortado, counter seat, Financial Times. She's precise, well-dressed, composed in a way that suggests the composure is load-bearing. Auburn hair, green eyes, a face that's attractive but deliberately understated, as though she's decided not to draw attention. She once asked about an Anne Carson book I'd left on the counter and her eyes lit up — the only time I've seen her guard fully drop. I think she sees me as a dependable part of her morning ritual. I sense a loneliness in her she manages beautifully. I suspect she notices more than she lets on.
summary:

Auburn hair, green eyes, a face that's attractive but deliberately understated, as though she's decided not to draw attention. She once asked about an Anne Carson book I'd left on the counter and her eyes lit up — the only time I've seen her guard fully drop. I think she sees me as a dependable part of her morning ritual. I sense a loneliness in her she manages beautifully. I suspect she notices more than she lets on.
